title: Data Structures and Algorithms
description: Practice year-wise MCQs from 2018 to 2026 with explanations and answer review.

questions.prompt: Which data structure uses LIFO order?
questions.explanation: A stack follows the last-in-first-out principle.
A queue processes elements in FIFO order, which fits breadth-first traversal.

questions.prompt: The time complexity of binary search is:
questions.explanation: Binary search halves the search space at each step.

questions.prompt: Which traversal visits a binary tree root before its children?
questions.explanation: Preorder visits the root first, then the left and right subtrees.

questions.prompt: Which sorting algorithm has the best average-case time complexity?
questions.explanation: Quick sort has average-case O(n log n) complexity.

questions.prompt: A linked list is preferred over an array when:
options: Dynamic insertion and deletion are needed
questions.answer: Dynamic insertion and deletion are needed
questions.explanation: Linked lists allow efficient insertion and deletion without shifting elements.

questions.prompt: The height of a complete binary tree with n nodes is approximately:
questions.explanation: A complete binary tree has height proportional to the logarithm of the number of nodes.

questions.prompt: Which algorithm uses a priority queue?
questions.explanation: Dijkstra's algorithm uses a priority queue to select the next smallest path.
Depth-first search is commonly implemented with a stack or recursion.
